title: Internal-External Mail Ratio
spl:
index=security tag=email
| eval ext=if(match(recipient,"@ourdomain\.com$"),0,1)
| timechart span=1h sum(ext) as ext_count, count as tot
| eval ext_ratio=ext_count/tot
description: Sudden flip to mostly external recipients may indicate data exfil via email or open relay abuse.
value: Sudden flip to mostly external recipients may indicate data exfil via email or open relay abuse.
implementation: Baseline per department; alert when `ext_ratio` >2× normal.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email by All_Email.action, All_Email.src_user span=1h | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Mail-volume and content shifts from marketing pushes, O365 throttling, mailbox migrations, and journaling changes; not every spike is a phishing wave.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Email CIM.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `recipient`, `src_user` with domain classification.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Baseline per department; alert when `ext_ratio` >2× normal.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=security tag=email
| eval ext=if(match(recipient,"@ourdomain\.com$"),0,1)
| timechart span=1h sum(ext) as ext_count, count as tot
| eval ext_ratio=ext_count/tot
```

Understanding this SPL

**Internal-External Mail Ratio** — Sudden flip to mostly external recipients may indicate data exfil via email or open relay abuse.

Documented **Data sources**: `recipient`, `src_user` with domain classification. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Email CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: security.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=security, tags. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ext** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ext_ratio** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Email.All_Email by All_Email.action, All_Email.src_user span=1h | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Internal-External Mail Ratio** — Sudden flip to mostly external recipients may indicate data exfil via email or open relay abuse.

Documented **Data sources**: `recipient`, `src_user` with domain classification. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Email CIM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Email.All_Email` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Accelerate the CIM data model used in the optional tstats path, then run both the primary and `cimSpl` searches over a week that includes a known good baseline. Spot-check a few rows and tune thresholds; confirm no permission or tagging gap is hiding part of the population.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Area chart (external ratio), Line chart.
grandmaExplanation: We use the same kind of check described for "Internal-External Mail Ratio" once our data is shaped in a common, repeatable way across products. We help the team see trouble early, compare different tools fairly, and avoid building a one-off report for every vendor.
dataModelAcceleration: Enable Data Model Acceleration for the CIM `Email.All_Email` dataset after normalizing this feed to the model; otherwise the `cimSpl` tstats search may return no rows.
